# SELECT after upload returns CSV with rows.
# We seed via INSERT DATA in a single fixture (POST update) — but
# every fixture starts fresh, so here we do a SELECT against an
# inserted-via-FORM body in the same request: actually, this fixture
# only validates header on empty store. The shape is the same as
# csv_empty but ensures CSV header bytes use \r\n and the spec
# header line is exactly empty when no bindings.
endpoint: sparql_query
classification: spec-required
match_mode: bytes-prefix
description: CSV results: empty SELECT just emits header line then \r\n

=== request
GET /sparql?query=SELECT%20%2A%20WHERE%20%7B%20%3Fs%20%3Fp%20%3Fo%20%7D%20LIMIT%200
Accept: text/csv

=== response
200
Content-Type: text/csv
